text: This video demonstrates laparoscopic right hemicolectomy with intracorporeal anastomosis for right colon cancer by Dr. Andrew Iraniha. Colon cancer is located in mid-ascending colon.

The surgeon identifies and ligates the ileocolic vessels using cautery and a buoyant energy device. The dissection proceeds medially to laterally towards the right upper quadrant and hepatic flexure, with the right ureter identified and protected. The right colic vessels and duodenum are identified, and the right colic vessels are ligated and divided. The hepatic flexure is fully mobilized, and the attachments around the duodenum are released to protect it. The right ureter is identified again, and the lateral peritoneal attachments of the right colon are mobilized.

The terminal ileum is positioned adjacent to the transverse colon and secured with a stay stitch. An enterotomy is created on both the terminal ileum and transverse colon. An anastomosis is formed between these structures using an Endo GIA stapler with two firings. The opening of the anastomosis is then closed with a 2-0 PDS suture in a simple running fashion.

The surgeon reinforces the suture line with Turo Etaban in a simple running fashion, followed by reinforcement of the paper line with Tulo Ekevat using a simple interrupted technique. The mesenteric defect is closed primarily with a two-hole echobon in a simple running manner.

The post-operative course was unremarkable. The patient was started on a clear liquid diet on post-op day number two and advanced to the pre-liquid diet on post-op day number three. She went home and followed up as an outpatient without any complications.
